problem_statement: How to sustain quality oversight of AI-generated code across long-horizon, continuous multi-agent software development, where agents both produce and judge their own output and human review must scale asynchronously over days of unattended operation.
magnitude_evidence: Evaluated over four long-running projects completing 164 milestones and 616 agent cycles spanning 137 wall-clock hours without human intervention beyond initial configuration; RustLaTex alone required 392 cycles and 95 milestones
insufficiency_evidence: "Apollo's unit-test gate can miss CI-level regressions, making Athena's planning cycle a necessary second quality layer"; also "Athena misclassified M92's heading-level variant as safe because the prior anti-pattern was documented as 'per-paragraph,' a distinction that a bounded context cannot reliably track over 90+ milestones"
solution_mechanisms: independent adversarial verification phase demanding concrete evidence
separation of producer and judge roles
asynchronous human oversight via issue filing
budget-halving fix rounds with exponential decay
budget-aware scheduling with rolling spend limit
persistent project state and knowledge base
self-aware termination on exhausted hypothesis space
